headline: Micron surges 15% on memory crunch, revenue quadruples
detail: Micron's blowout print confirms AI-driven memory demand is crimping supply hard and pricing is spiking. This is directly positive for SMH (your only green chip holding) and gives AVGO and CRWD a sector tailwind today. Watch if it translates into actual price recovery in your red positions.

headline: Qualcomm stock soars on strong numbers
detail: Another major semi name beating expectations reinforces the chip sector narrative going into the back half of the year. Good backdrop for SMH and potentially supportive for AVGO on any bounce. Doesn't fix your cost basis but makes the sector less scary right now.

headline: White House requests $87.6B supplemental spending for Iran war
detail: Large emergency defense spending signals the Iran conflict is escalating beyond a quick resolution. Macro uncertainty like this tends to pressure growth and tech multiples, which is a headwind for your concentrated tech/chip book. Keep an eye on how markets digest this alongside the semi earnings optimism.

headline: Cerebras stock sinks below IPO price
detail: A high-profile AI chip IPO rolling over is a cautionary data point for your two new IPO positions, especially SPCX and SPAL. New listings in hot themes can unravel fast; this reinforces why SPCX needs manual management and SPAL's stop deserves serious respect on margin.
